analysis.note: Technical class-name used substantively/adjectivally with caitasaiḥ: “universal/all-pervasive” mental factors.

verification.detail: compound stem sarvatraga as Pum/Trtiya/Bahu: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Nominalized past participial adjective from vi-ni-√yam, here a technical class-name: “determinate/restricted” mental factors.
Technical ethical class-name: wholesome mental factors.
Feminine nominative singular, agreeing with saṃprayuktā and trivedanā; the antecedent is supplied from the preceding verse-context.

Past passive participial adjective from sam-pra-√yuj, predicative with asau; it governs the instrumental plurals. The pausal/verifier-normalized form is samprayuktā; surface_in_sandhi preserves the printed saṃprayuktā.
Connective adverb: “likewise/also,” extending the saṃprayuktā-construction to kleśaiḥ and upakleśaiḥ.
Technical term: secondary or subsidiary afflictions.

Predicative feminine nominative singular of the whole compound stem trivedana: “having three feelings,” not two separate words tri + vedanā. Corrected from an ā-stem lemma trivedanā to the a-stem adjectival compound trivedana. The bahuvrīhi relation is retained; the verifier only logged that relation as unsupported, not derivationally disproved.

verification.detail: compound stem trivedana as Stri/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

justifications.decision: The series beginning with sarvatragaiḥ is not translated merely as ordinary adjectives such as “all-pervading, restrained, good” nor kleśa as generic “pain”; Sthiramati treats the items beginning with sarvatraga as headings already mentioned and requiring subsequent display.
options_considered: Translate sarvatraga, viniyata, kuśala, kleśa, upakleśa as ordinary non-technical descriptors.
Translate them as technical class-headings in a caitasa/caitta taxonomy.
justifications.chosen: technical class-names: universal, determinate, wholesome mental factors, afflictions, and secondary afflictions

evidence.quote: ya ete sarvatragādaya uddiṣṭās te na vijñāyanta ity atas tatpradarśanārtham
evidence.translation: Since these, beginning with the sarvatragas, have been stated but are not understood, therefore, for the purpose of displaying them...

justifications.decision: The verse says that the feminine subject is “conjoined/associated” with the listed classes. tathā is therefore taken as “likewise/also,” adding kleśaiḥ and upakleśaiḥ to the same instrumental construction rather than starting an independent clause.
options_considered: Construe only caitasaiḥ with saṃprayuktā and leave kleśaiḥ/upakleśaiḥ syntactically separate.
Construe the whole list of instrumentals with saṃprayuktā, tathā adding the later items.
justifications.chosen: all the instrumental plurals are governed by saṃprayuktā, with tathā continuing the same construction

evidence.quote: sarvatragair viniyataiḥ kuśalaiś caitasair asau | saṃprayuktā tathā kleśair upakleśais trivedanā
evidence.translation: With universal, determinate, and wholesome mental factors this is conjoined; likewise with afflictions and secondary afflictions; it has three feelings.

evidence.quote: ya ete sarvatragādaya uddiṣṭās te na vijñāyanta
evidence.translation: These, beginning with the sarvatragas, have been mentioned but are not understood.

justifications.decision: Although asau can be masculine or feminine in form, the feminine nominative predicates saṃprayuktā and trivedanā require the feminine reading here.
options_considered: asau as nominative masculine singular, as in the analyzer output.
asau as nominative feminine singular agreeing with saṃprayuktā and trivedanā.
justifications.chosen: asau is feminine nominative singular, with saṃprayuktā and trivedanā as feminine predicates

evidence.translation: this [feminine] is conjoined with mental factors ... [and is] three-feelinged.

justifications.decision: The printed verse has trivedanā as one word, and its feminine nominative singular form agrees predicatively with asau/saṃprayuktā. It is therefore not split as tri + vedanā, nor taken as an independent substantive “the three feelings.”
options_considered: Split tri and vedanā as two words.
Take trivedanā as a dvigu/tatpuruṣa substantive meaning “the three feelings.”
Take trivedanā as a bahuvrīhi adjective: “having three feelings.”
justifications.chosen: trivedanā is the feminine nominative singular of the bahuvrīhi adjective stem trivedana: “having three feelings”

evidence.translation: conjoined likewise with afflictions and secondary afflictions, [it is] three-feelinged.

translation:
With universal (sarvatraga), determinate (viniyata),
and wholesome mental factors (caitasa), this [apprehension] is conjoined;
likewise with afflictions (kleśa) and secondary afflictions (upakleśa):
it has three feelings (tri-vedanā).
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind rendering could treat sarvatraga, viniyata, kuśala, kleśa, and upakleśa as ordinary dictionary words; Sthiramati’s sarvatragādaya uddiṣṭāḥ shows they are technical headings to be displayed next.
It could follow the analyzer and make asau masculine; the verse’s feminine predicates require a feminine subject.
It could split trivedanā into tri + vedanā and translate “three feelings” as an independent noun phrase; the verse requires a predicative compound, “having three feelings.”
It could over-explain the individual members of these classes from the following verse; line 493 marks verse 9 only as the uddesa/listing whose pradarśana begins next.
analyzer_disagreements: viniyataiḥ: the analyzer lemmatized it under viniyam; here it is analyzed as the nominal stem viniyata, Pum Trtiya Bahu, a technical class-name in the verse’s list.
asau: the analyzer marked nominative masculine singular; agreement with saṃprayuktā and trivedanā requires nominative feminine singular of adas.

saṃprayuktā: treated here as a subanta past passive participial adjective from sam-pra-√yuj with verifier-normalized stem samprayukta, not as a finite verbal form; the printed saṃ- is retained in surface_in_sandhi.

tri_vedanā: the analyzer split the printed trivedanā into tri + vedanā; here it is one compound, feminine nominative singular of the adjectival compound stem trivedana, used predicatively as a bahuvrīhi.
open_questions: The supplied commentary on this verse does not define the individual items within the sarvatraga and following classes; that exposition begins in the lookahead to the next verse and is not used here as verse-9 evidence.
The explicit antecedent of asau lies outside this verse’s supplied span; the translation supplies “[apprehension]” only to make the feminine demonstrative intelligible in English.
